fault_hypothesis: Lines 68-69 incorrectly pop the last element when the last elements of both inputs equal the last element of merged, which removes a valid duplicate entry and reduces the output length by 1.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The spurious pop condition should never be true for valid merged output; when it triggers it incorrectly removes a legitimate element.
After initialization merged must be empty before any elements are appended.
After the conditional pop the merged length must still equal the sum of input lengths; popping violates this invariant.
At each loop iteration the merged list length must equal i+j (total elements consumed so far).
The returned merged list must contain exactly len(left)+len(right) elements.
